etymology.description: 玨(玨) (jué, "pieces of jade") + 刀(刀) (dāo, "knife") -> to cut jade into pieces with a knife -> shift; work
etymology.interpretation: The character 班(bān, "shift; work") originally depicted the action of cutting a piece of jade into parts with a knife. Just as jade is divided into smaller parts, work or tasks are divided into shifts or segments, bridging the concept of dividing valuable items and dividing time or tasks.

examples.chinese: 我今天的班(我今天的班)结束后我们去喝饮料(結束後我們去喝飲料)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ jīntiān de bān jiéshù hòu wǒmen qù hē yǐnliào.
examples.english: After my shift ends today, we will go for drinks.

examples.chinese: 他新换了一个班(他新換了一個班)，觉得很适应(覺得很適應)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xīn huànle yī gè bān, juéde hěn shìyìng.
examples.english: He has switched to a new shift and feels very adapted to it.

examples.chinese: 这个班(這個班)的工作时间很长(的工作時間很長)，但是报酬不错(但是報酬不錯)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège bān de gōngzuò shíjiān hěn cháng, dànshì bàochóu bùcuò.
examples.english: This shift has long working hours, but the pay is good.
